page: Amazon Web Services c5a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor, delivering 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The dedicated processor operates at a speed of 3.3 GHz and includes L1d, L2, and L3 caches of 32 KB, 0.5 MB, and 16 MB respectively. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals distinct performance characteristics and trade-offs. Single-core performance is a primary bottleneck, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the bottom 10%. Conversely, multi-core capabilities are highly competitive. The passmark CPU encryption test reaches the top 10% tier, while other multi-core and specialized benchmarks, such as software compilation with clang, ray tracing, image processing, and passmark database operations, rank in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong, though large block read and write bandwidth remains average. LLM inference speeds are strong for small models but drop to average for medium and large models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5a.24xlarge leverages AMD EPYC processors to deliver high compute density and strong multi-threaded performance without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPU hardware. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly efficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded software compilation, cryptography and encryption tasks, ray tracing, image processing, and database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c5a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R32 processor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is established at a baseline of 20 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is poor, placing in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-threaded operations are strong. Specifically, CPU encryption benchmarks rank in the top 10%, and software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations place in the top 25%. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-dense, multi-threaded tasks that do not require local storage. It is suited for workloads such as software compilation, cryptographic processing, image processing, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 20 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core encryption and compilation performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring ninety-six AMD EPYC vCPUs for high-density multi-threaded processing and cryptographic workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor
192.0 GB of DDR4 memory with a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps
Top-tier performance in CPU encryption and strong results in software compilation
Tradeoff of poor single-core CPU performance offset by strong multi-core execution
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for multi-threaded compilation, cryptography, and database operations
